name: VSM Financial Converter
description: Converts Value Stream Mapping operational metrics into financial impact, highlighting hidden losses and improvement opportunities based on Lean, Six Sigma, and ISO standards.

inputs.label: Annual Demand (units)
inputs.businessContext: Total customer demand per year for the product family under analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total customer demand per year for the product family under analysis.

inputs.label: Selling Price per unit
inputs.businessContext: Average selling price per unit to the customer.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average selling price per unit to the customer.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per hour
inputs.businessContext: Fully loaded labor cost per operator hour.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fully loaded labor cost per operator hour.

inputs.label: Total Cycle Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all process cycle times for one unit through the value stream.

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of all process cycle times for one unit through the value stream.

inputs.label: Total Value-Added Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of only value-added process times (ISO 22400, Lean definition).

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of only value-added process times (ISO 22400, Lean definition).

inputs.label: Defect Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of units that require rework or are scrapped (First Pass Yield).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of units that require rework or are scrapped (First Pass Yield).

inputs.label: Rework Cost per defective unit
inputs.businessContext: Additional cost to rework one defective unit (labor + material).

businessContext_i18n.en: Additional cost to rework one defective unit (labor + material).

inputs.label: Inventory Holding Cost (% of COGS)
inputs.businessContext: Annual inventory carrying cost as a percentage of cost of goods sold (WERC standard).

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual inventory carrying cost as a percentage of cost of goods sold (WERC standard).

inputs.label: Average Inventory Value (currency)
inputs.businessContext: Average value of raw material, WIP, and finished goods inventory.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average value of raw material, WIP, and finished goods inventory.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate (% of direct labor)
inputs.businessContext: Manufacturing overhead allocated as a percentage of direct labor cost.

businessContext_i18n.en: Manufacturing overhead allocated as a percentage of direct labor cost.

inputs.label: Operators per shift
inputs.businessContext: Number of direct operators working per shift in the value stream.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of direct operators working per shift in the value stream.

inputs.label: Shifts per day
inputs.businessContext: Number of production shifts per day.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of production shifts per day.

inputs.label: Working days per year
inputs.businessContext: Number of operating days per year.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of operating days per year.

inputs.label: Waste Reduction Target (%)
inputs.businessContext: Target percentage reduction in non-value-added time (Lean continuous improvement).

businessContext_i18n.en: Target percentage reduction in non-value-added time (Lean continuous improvement).

rules.message: Value-added time cannot exceed total cycle time.
Defect rate must be between 0 and 100.
Average inventory value must be non-negative.
Waste reduction target must be between 0 and 100.

thresholds.message: Defect rate exceeds 5% – significant quality loss.
Value-added ratio below 30% – high non-value-added waste.
Inventory value exceeds 1M currency – consider reduction initiatives.

formulas.description: Direct labor cost per unit based on cycle time and labor rate.
Overhead cost per unit allocated as percentage of direct labor.
Cost of poor quality per unit (defect rate * rework cost).
Inventory holding cost per unit based on average inventory and demand.
Total manufacturing cost per unit (material + labor + overhead + quality + inventory).
Profit per unit before waste reduction.
Annual financial loss due to non-value-added time (waste).
Primary result: Annual profit after accounting for waste loss and improvement potential.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive Non-Value-Added Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: The ratio of NVA time to total cycle time is high, indicating process inefficiencies.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Defect Rework
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Defects consume resources without adding value, increasing cost per unit.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Inventory Carrying Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Excess inventory ties up capital and incurs holding costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Overhead Allocation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: High overhead rate relative to direct labor may indicate indirect waste.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Kaizen on bottleneck processes
suggestedActions.description: Focus on reducing cycle time of the highest NVA process step to improve throughput.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduction in total cycle time by 10-20%

suggestedActions.label: Deploy Six Sigma DMAIC for defect reduction
suggestedActions.description: Identify root causes of defects and implement controls to reduce defect rate.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Defect rate reduction by 50% or more

suggestedActions.label: Introduce Kanban system to reduce WIP inventory
suggestedActions.description: Pull-based inventory control to lower average inventory value.

suggestedActions.label: Review overhead allocation and eliminate non-value-adding activities
suggestedActions.description: Lean office and indirect process improvement to reduce overhead rate.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Profit
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.0) based on input data completeness and reliability.
